name: 1Panel MCP Server
what_it_does: Official 1Panel MCP server enabling AI agents to interact with 1Panel — the popular open-source Linux server management panel — managing websites, databases, Docker containers, system resources, and files through 1Panel's REST API, integrating server administration into agent-driven DevOps workflows.
use_cases: Managing websites and web servers from deployment agents
Controlling Docker containers and applications from DevOps agents
Monitoring server resource usage from infrastructure agents
Managing databases (MySQL, PostgreSQL) from database admin agents
File management on remote servers from administration agents
Automating server maintenance tasks from operations agents
not_for: Teams not using 1Panel for server management
Teams using cPanel, Plesk, or other panels (use respective MCPs)
Direct Linux server access without 1Panel installed
best_when: A DevOps team uses 1Panel to manage Linux servers and wants AI-assisted administration — automating server management, container orchestration, and website deployment through 1Panel's interface.
avoid_when: You don't use 1Panel or prefer direct SSH/shell access for server management.

auth.notes: 1Panel API key required. Generate from 1Panel admin panel. API key provides access to 1Panel management functions.

pricing.free_tier_limits: 1Panel is completely free and open source
pricing.notes: 1Panel is a free open-source Linux server panel. Official MCP server is also free. Server hosting costs apply.

performance.rate_limits: Local 1Panel API — limited by server resources

known_agent_gotchas: 1Panel must be installed and running on target server
API key must be generated in 1Panel admin — not automatic
Server management operations (restart services, delete containers) are irreversible
Official MCP from 1Panel-dev org — higher quality than typical community MCPs
API changes with 1Panel versions — keep MCP and panel in sync
TLS certificate for 1Panel API endpoint may be self-signed
agent_readiness.security_notes: Server management API — treat API key as root-level credential. Use HTTPS. Official MCP. Restrict API key permissions where possible. Server operations affect production systems.

security_score_components.security_notes: Server admin API key. HTTPS required. Official MCP. Server operations are highly privileged.
